root_text.an8.8:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā uttaro mahisavatthusmiṁ viharati saṅkheyyake pabbate vaṭajālikāyaṁ.
root_text.an8.8:1.2: Tatra kho āyasmā uttaro bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an8.8:1.3: “sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attavipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
root_text.an8.8:1.4: Sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ paravipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
root_text.an8.8:1.5: Sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attasampattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
root_text.an8.8:1.6: Sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ parasampattiṁ paccavekkhitā hotī”ti.
root_text.an8.8:2.1: Tena kho pana samayena vessavaṇo mahārājā uttarāya disāya dakkhiṇaṁ disaṁ gacchati kenacideva karaṇīyena.
root_text.an8.8:2.2: Assosi kho vessavaṇo mahārājā āyasmato uttarassa mahisavatthusmiṁ saṅkheyyake pabbate vaṭajālikāyaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ desentassa:
root_text.an8.8:2.3: “sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attavipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
root_text.an8.8:2.4: Sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ paravipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
root_text.an8.8:2.5: Sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attasampattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
root_text.an8.8:2.6: Sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ parasampattiṁ paccavekkhitā hotī”ti.
root_text.an8.8:3.1: Atha kho vessavaṇo mahārājā—seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso samiñjitaṁ vā bāhaṁ pasāreyya, pasāritaṁ vā bāhaṁ samiñjeyya; evamevaṁ mahisavatthusmiṁ saṅkheyyake pabbate vaṭajālikāyaṁ antarahito devesu tāvatiṁsesu pāturahosi.
root_text.an8.8:3.2: Atha kho vessavaṇo mahārājā yena sakko devānamindo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā sakkaṁ devānamindaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an8.8:3.4: Eso āyasmā uttaro mahisavatthusmiṁ saṅkheyyake pabbate vaṭajālikāyaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.an8.8:3.5: ‘sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attavipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
root_text.an8.8:4.1: Atha kho sakko devānamindo seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso samiñjitaṁ vā bāhaṁ pasāreyya, pasāritaṁ vā bāhaṁ samiñjeyya; evamevaṁ devesu tāvatiṁsesu antarahito mahisavatthusmiṁ saṅkheyyake pabbate vaṭajālikāyaṁ āyasmato uttarassa sammukhe pāturahosi.
root_text.an8.8:4.2: Atha kho sakko devānamindo yenāyasmā uttaro tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ uttaraṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhito kho sakko devānamindo āyasmantaṁ uttaraṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an8.8:5.1: “Saccaṁ kira, bhante, āyasmā uttaro bhikkhūnaṁ evaṁ dhammaṁ desesi:
root_text.an8.8:5.2: ‘sādhāvuso, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attavipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti,
root_text.an8.8:5.7: “Kiṁ panidaṁ, bhante, āyasmato uttarassa sakaṁ paṭibhānaṁ, udāhu tassa bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassā”ti?
root_text.an8.8:5.9: Upamāya m’idhekacce viññū purisā bhāsitassa atthaṁ ājānanti.
root_text.an8.8:6.1: Seyyathāpi, devānaminda, gāmassa vā nigamassa vā avidūre mahādhaññarāsi.
root_text.an8.8:6.4: Yo nu kho, devānaminda, taṁ mahājanakāyaṁ upasaṅkamitvā evaṁ puccheyya:
root_text.an8.8:6.5: ‘kuto imaṁ dhaññaṁ āharathā’ti, kathaṁ byākaramāno nu kho, devānaminda, so mahājanakāyo sammā byākaramāno byākareyyā”ti?
root_text.an8.8:6.6: “‘Amumhā mahādhaññarāsimhā āharāmā’ti kho, bhante, so mahājanakāyo sammā byākaramāno byākareyyā”ti.
root_text.an8.8:6.7: “Evamevaṁ kho, devānaminda, yaṁ kiñci subhāsitaṁ sabbaṁ taṁ tassa bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassa.
root_text.an8.8:6.8: Tato upādāyupādāya mayaṁ caññe ca bhaṇāmā”ti.
root_text.an8.8:7.3: ‘yaṁ kiñci subhāsitaṁ sabbaṁ taṁ tassa bhagavato vacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassa.
root_text.an8.8:7.5: Ekamidaṁ, bhante uttara, samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati gijjhakūṭe pabbate acirapakkante devadatte.
root_text.an8.8:7.6: Tatra kho bhagavā devadattaṁ ārabbha bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an8.8:8.1: ‘Sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ attavipattiṁ paccavekkhitā hoti.
root_text.an8.8:8.2: Sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kālena kālaṁ paravipattiṁ …pe…
root_text.an8.8:8.5: Aṭṭhahi, bhikkhave, asaddhammehi abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto devadatto āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho.
root_text.an8.8:8.7: Lābhena hi, bhikkhave, abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto devadatto āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho;
root_text.an8.8:8.14: pāpamittatāya, bhikkhave, abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto devadatto āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho.
root_text.an8.8:8.15: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, aṭṭhahi asaddhammehi abhibhūto pariyādinnacitto devadatto āpāyiko nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho.
root_text.an8.8:9.1: Sādhu, bhikkhave, bhikkhu uppannaṁ lābhaṁ abhibhuyya abhibhuyya vihareyya;
root_text.an8.8:10.1: Kiñca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca uppannaṁ lābhaṁ abhibhuyya abhibhuyya vihareyya;
root_text.an8.8:11.1: Yaṁ hissa, bhikkhave, uppannaṁ lābhaṁ anabhibhuyya viharato uppajjeyyuṁ āsavā vighātapariḷāhā, uppannaṁ lābhaṁ abhibhuyya viharato evaṁsa te āsavā vighātapariḷāhā na honti.
root_text.an8.8:11.8: uppannaṁ pāpamittataṁ anabhibhuyya viharato uppajjeyyuṁ āsavā vighātapariḷāhā, uppannaṁ pāpamittataṁ abhibhuyya viharato evaṁsa te āsavā vighātapariḷāhā na honti.
root_text.an8.8:11.9: Idaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu atthavasaṁ paṭicca uppannaṁ lābhaṁ abhibhuyya abhibhuyya vihareyya;
root_text.an8.8:13.1: Ettāvatā, bhante uttara, manussesu catasso parisā—
root_text.an8.8:13.4: Uggaṇhatu, bhante, āyasmā uttaro imaṁ dhammapariyāyaṁ.
root_text.an8.8:13.5: Pariyāpuṇātu, bhante, āyasmā uttaro imaṁ dhammapariyāyaṁ.
root_text.an8.8:13.6: Dhāretu, bhante, āyasmā uttaro imaṁ dhammapariyāyaṁ.

translation_text.an8.8:1.1: At one time Venerable Uttara was staying on the Saṅkheyyaka Mountain in the Mahisa region near Dhavajālikā.
translation_text.an8.8:1.3: “Mendicants, it’s good for a mendicant to check their own failings from time to time.
translation_text.an8.8:1.4: It’s good for a mendicant to check the failings of others from time to time.
translation_text.an8.8:1.5: It’s good for a mendicant to check their own successes from time to time.
translation_text.an8.8:1.6: It’s good for a mendicant to check the successes of others from time to time.”
translation_text.an8.8:2.1: Now at that time the great king Vessavaṇa was on his way from the north to the south on some business.
translation_text.an8.8:2.2: He heard Venerable Uttara teaching this to the mendicants on Saṅkheyyaka Mountain.
translation_text.an8.8:3.1: Then Vessavaṇa vanished from Saṅkheyyaka Mountain and appeared among the gods of the thirty-three, as easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm.
translation_text.an8.8:3.2: Then he went up to Sakka, lord of gods, and said to him:
translation_text.an8.8:3.3: “Please good fellow, you should know this.
translation_text.an8.8:3.4: Venerable Uttara is teaching the mendicants on Saṅkheyyaka Mountain in this way:
translation_text.an8.8:3.5: ‘It’s good for a mendicant from time to time to check their own failings. …
translation_text.an8.8:4.1: Then, as easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm, Sakka vanished from the gods of the thirty-three and reappeared on Saṅkheyyaka Mountain in front of Venerable Uttara.
translation_text.an8.8:4.2: Then Sakka went up to Venerable Uttara, bowed, stood to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an8.8:5.1: “Is it really true, sir, that you teach the mendicants in this way:
translation_text.an8.8:5.2: ‘It’s good for a mendicant from time to time to check their own failings …
translation_text.an8.8:5.7: “Sir, did this teaching come to you from your own inspiration, or was it spoken by the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha?”
translation_text.an8.8:5.8: “Well then, lord of gods, I shall give you a simile.
translation_text.an8.8:5.9: For by means of a simile some sensible people understand the meaning of what is said.
translation_text.an8.8:6.1: Suppose there was a large heap of grain not far from a town or village.
translation_text.an8.8:6.2: And a large crowd were to take away grain
translation_text.an8.8:6.3: with carrying poles, baskets, hip sacks, or their cupped hands.
translation_text.an8.8:6.4: If someone were to go to that crowd and ask them
translation_text.an8.8:6.5: where they got the grain from, how should that crowd rightly reply?”
translation_text.an8.8:6.6: “Sir, they should reply that they took it from the large heap of grain.”
translation_text.an8.8:6.7: “In the same way, lord of gods, whatever is well spoken is spoken by the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.an8.8:6.8: Both myself and others rely completely on that when we speak.”
translation_text.an8.8:7.2: How well this was said by Venerable Uttara!
translation_text.an8.8:7.3: ‘Whatever is well spoken is spoken by the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.an8.8:7.4: Both myself and others rely completely on that when we speak.’
translation_text.an8.8:7.5: At one time, Honorable Uttara, the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, on the Vulture’s Peak Mountain, not long after Devadatta had left.
translation_text.an8.8:7.6: There the Buddha spoke to the mendicants about Devadatta:
translation_text.an8.8:8.1: ‘Mendicants, it’s good for a mendicant from time to time to check their own failings …
translation_text.an8.8:8.5: Overcome and overwhelmed by eight things that oppose the true teaching, Devadatta is going to a place of loss, to hell, there to remain for an eon, irredeemable.
translation_text.an8.8:8.14: bad friendship, Devadatta is going to a place of loss, to hell, there to remain for an eon, irredeemable.
translation_text.an8.8:8.15: Overcome and overwhelmed by these eight things that oppose the true teaching, Devadatta is going to a place of loss, to hell, there to remain for an eon, irredeemable.
translation_text.an8.8:9.1: It’s good for a mendicant, whenever they encounter it, to overcome gain …
translation_text.an8.8:10.1: What advantage does a mendicant gain by overcoming these eight things?
translation_text.an8.8:11.1: The distressing and feverish defilements that might arise in someone who lives without overcoming these eight things do not arise when they have overcome them.
translation_text.an8.8:11.9: This is the advantage that a mendicant gains by overcoming these eight things.
translation_text.an8.8:12.1: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an8.8:12.2: “Whenever we encounter it, we will overcome gain …
translation_text.an8.8:13.1: Honorable Uttara, this exposition of the teaching is not established anywhere in the four assemblies—
translation_text.an8.8:13.4: Sir, learn this exposition of the teaching!
translation_text.an8.8:13.5: Memorize this exposition of the teaching!
translation_text.an8.8:13.6: Remember this exposition of the teaching!
translation_text.an8.8:13.7: Sir, this exposition of the teaching is beneficial and relevant to the fundamentals of the spiritual life.”

variant_text.an8.8:3.1: samiñjitaṁ → sammiñjitaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed) | samiñjeyya → sammiñjeyya (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
variant_text.an8.8:5.7: panidaṁ → kiṁ pana (sya-all) | sakaṁ paṭibhānaṁ → sakaṁ paṭibhāṇaṁ (sya-all); sakapaṭibhānaṁ upādāya (mr)
